review_notes: The functions accurately map to Kubernetes RBAC controls and align with ISO27001 A.5.2 on roles and responsibilities. Naming conventions are mostly followed, but a minor change was made to ensure consistency with the pattern used for other functions.
naming_improvements: Use 'enabled' instead of 'configured' to align with existing naming conventions
title: Roles and Responsibilities
step1_reasoning: The ISO27001 control A.5.2 focuses on defining roles and responsibilities, which aligns with Kubernetes RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) mechanisms. These mechanisms ensure that users and service accounts have the appropriate roles and permissions, enforcing the principle of least privilege. The functions generated address key aspects of RBAC, such as preventing the use of cluster-admin bindings, ensuring least privilege, and managing service account tokens. Additionally, configuring OIDC authentication in Kubernetes can be related to enforcing identity and access management policies similar to MFA in cloud environments.
